name: Conjugate gradient method
task: Create /tmp/eval_cg/cg.py with conjugate_gradient(A, b, x0=None, tol=1e-10, max_iter=None) solving Ax=b for symmetric positive-definite A using the standard CG iteration: r = b - Ax; p = r; loop: alpha = (r·r)/(p·Ap); x += alpha*p; r' = r - alpha*Ap; if |r'|² < tol² break; beta = (r'·r')/(r·r); p = r' + beta*p. Default x0 is zero vector; default max_iter = 2n.

validation.script:
import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_cg/cg.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); CG = mod.conjugate_gradient; A = [[4,1],[1,3]]; b = [1,2]; x = CG(A, b); assert abs(x[0] - 1/11) < 1e-9 and abs(x[1] - 7/11) < 1e-9, x; n = 4; I = [[1.0 if i==j else 0.0 for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)]; bb = [3.14, 2.72, 1.41, 1.62]; xx = CG(I, bb)
for i in range(n): assert abs(xx[i] - bb[i]) < 1e-9
print('OK')
